#### Queries 1–2 · Condition — AMI Diagnoses

> Two queries are used because combining very long comma-separated code lists across systems can exceed server URL length limits. Union the `Condition.subject` references from both.

#### Query 3 · Observation — Cardiac Troponin I Threshold

| Parameter | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| LOINC | `10839-9` | Troponin I.cardiac \[Mass/vol\] in Serum or Plasma |
| `value-quantity` | `ge0.04` ng/mL | 99th-percentile URL per universal MI definition; PheKB AMI-confirmation threshold |
| `status` | `final,amended,corrected` | Excludes preliminary / entered-in-error results |

> ⚠️ If your institution uses **high-sensitivity troponin I** (LOINC `89579-7`), add a second Observation query with that code and the assay-specific 99th-percentile URL (typically ~0.006 ng/mL for hsTnI).

| Parameter | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|

| `status` | `active,completed` | Excludes cancelled / entered-in-error orders |

eligible_patients =

  ∪ subjects(ConditionQuery_ICD10)
  ∪ subjects(ObservationQuery_TroponinI)

| Layer | Detail |
|---|---|

| **Clinical-drug-form codes** | `617310` (10 mg), `617311` (20 mg), `617312` (40 mg), `617318` (80 mg) — the four standard tablet strengths used in BioVU pull lists. |

| **`_include=MedicationRequest:subject`** | Returns the linked `Patient` resource in one round-trip for cohort extraction. |
| **180-day temporal rule** | Query 5 co-locates AMI conditions with the Rx pull; you must post-process `MedicationRequest.authoredOn` vs. `Condition.onsetDateTime` to enforce the ≥ 180-day prior-exposure window required by the PheKB algorithm. |
